title: fritillaria messanensis subsp. atlantica
intro: Fritillaria messanensis subsp. atlantica, also known as the Atlas Fritillaria, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The plant contains compounds like saponins and alkaloids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its health benefits, including the relief of congestion and the soothing of irritated throats. Fritillaria messanensis subsp. atlantica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for oral consumption, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a variety of health conditions.
